name: Old Fashioned Meat Sauce for Pasta
recipeInstructions:
1. Blanch and peel half of the tomatoes.
2. Place in a slow cooker with half of the olive oil, marjoram, oregano, sugar, salt, sweet basil, and minced garlic.
3. Slowly simmer for 24 to 48 hours or until the tomato solids are completely reduced.
4. Blanch and peel the remaining tomatoes, remove seeds, and puree.
5. Add to the slow cooker along with the tomato paste and continue cooking slowly for another 24 hours.
6. Saute the ground beef in a pan and add to the slow cooker with the remainder of the olive oil.
7. Continue cooking for at least half a day.
8. During the last four hours of cooking, add shredded Parmesan cheese and blend well.
9. Add mushrooms about 15 minutes before serving.
10. If making meatballs, add them with the cheese and remove before serving.
11. Serve the sauce over or alongside pasta.
